item.title: Operational Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
Wikimedia links link only to the image themselves, not licensing info

First one is by JJ Harrison, under CC BY-SA 3.0.

Second image is by Adam Kumiszcza, under CC BY-SA 3.0.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love the concept and execution - it's so weird, but also endearing.

The final iteration made me sad, though. D:

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There is that joke about penguins flying around when no one is looking… there was even an animation short about it.

The ending should really about the "flyers" being a double entendre, and the secrets of flying penguins never being discovered by the fundation while making it unclear if the penguin is joking or not.

That'd add +50 or something.

Good shit basir. Back when I critiqued this even on its conceptual level, I just knew that this idea had merit, and could make for a really nice read; it just needed some tweaking regarding its narrative focus to make it more impactful than what it was before. Overall, a great job.

I'm glad to have critiqued this in its conceptual phase and brought it to what it is now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I’m so happy to see this up! I’ll edit this comment with a more in-depth review soon, but it’s a +1 from me! Good job!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for catching it out, I'll quickly fix it

item.title: Re: Rieview
item.content:encoded:
Thank you for the critique! Yeah, I did had a hard time pacing out the chatlogs, should've cut them down a bit, but overall I was happy with how it turned out.

The ending was supposed to have more messages, but I decided that one post will suffice and since i think it would've detracted from the emotions of the post

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There is a small mistake on the first page, in the Culture of SCP-5451-1 Instances under Family Life. Near the end of that, it says SCP-XXXX-1.

Also +1. No question about it.

item.title: Rieview
item.content:encoded:
I can't believe this is up already!

Firstly - I think this is an amazing job for your first SCP. The formatting, the forums, offsets, usernames, everything is incredibly well done. I had to double check that you hadn't written anything before.

Gonna put this in a collapsible before a mod gets me for lengthy comments. :P

+ Rieview
- It's a pun!

So - here are the other positives.

The idea is cute, quirky, and handled well. The vector of communication, eg the forums and chat logs, feels natural and it allows us time to see Cook change in their motivation.
The pacing is also mostly good - 3 pages does feel long, but everything is interesting enough to sustain. That's not to say there aren't places where it doesn't drag, but I found it snappy enough.
And the humour is appropriately cute but never really hit me over the head with its quirkyness.

Now - things to work on in the future.
Pacing. There are… Many chatlogs. How many of them are necessary? What's their purpose? Every time you include one, that's a choice to balance pacing and humour. You need to make sure people are happy with the loss of pacing.

Ending. This wraps up really quickly. Somewhat to the detriment of the article. You build up too little for this let-down to be intentional, but too much for it to satisfy the reader. I'm at a loss for what happened.

Naming your addenda - super minor, but they are all variations on "increasing anomalous effects". I understand you want the logs themselves to reveal the information, but it's a bit repetitive.

A happy +1.

item.title: The best forums in the Arctic!
item.content:encoded:
After 3 days of labor. this is what i came out with.

Special thanks to the following people:

+ thank you to the people here
- your critiques were very helpful

chiifu for improving the intial concept and for the critique
DianaBerry and KaraKatt for the greenlights along with helping me improve the inital concept even further
Riemann for SPaG stuff, critique, encouragement
PlaguePJP for SPaG stuff and critique

I either used or modified the following images

+ Image Bibliography
- most of them are from flickr and CC-BY-SA 2.0


https://www.flickr.com/photos/95012874@N00/186727345
https://www.flickr.com/photos/28946988@N07/14736449505
